name: Rage
description: Bonus Action; self; requires not wearing Heavy Armor. While raging: Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws; Resistance to bludgeoning/piercing/slashing damage; you can’t cast spells or maintain Concentration. Rage Damage: when you hit with a Strength-based weapon attack or Unarmed Strike, add +2 damage (Barbarian levels 1–8), +3 (levels 9–15), or +4 (levels 16–20). Duration: lasts until the end of your next turn; ends early if you don Heavy Armor or have the Incapacitated condition. To keep raging past your next turn, on that next turn do at least one of: make an attack roll against an enemy, force an enemy to make a saving throw, or take a Bonus Action to extend; each extension carries duration to the end of your following turn. Max duration 10 minutes. Uses: 2 rages at level 1; 3 at level 3; 4 at level 6; 5 at level 12; 6 at level 17. Regain 1 expended use on a Short Rest; regain all on a Long Rest.

name: Unarmored Defense
description: Passive; while not wearing any armor, your AC is 10 + Dex modifier + Con modifier. You can still use a Shield and benefit.

name: Weapon Mastery
description: Passive; you can use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial melee weapons you choose. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those chosen weapon kinds. Number of weapon kinds you can master: 2 (Barbarian levels 1–3), 3 (levels 4–9), 4 (levels 10–20).

name: Relentless Rage
description: Trigger (no action required); if you drop to 0 HP while your Rage is active and don’t die outright, make a DC 10 Constitution save. On a success, your HP becomes twice your Barbarian level instead of 0. Each time you use this feature after the first (before you rest), the DC increases by 5; the DC resets to 10 when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

name: Improved Brutal Strike
description: Passive upgrade; you add new Brutal Strike effect options: (1) Staggering Blow—target has Disadvantage on its next saving throw and can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn; (2) Sundering Blow—before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains +5 (only one Sundering bonus can apply to a roll).

name: Persistent Rage
description: Passive + trigger; your Rage can last the full 10 minutes without needing to extend it round-by-round. It still ends early if you don Heavy Armor or have the Unconscious condition. In addition, when you roll Initiative you can regain all expended Rage uses; once you do, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Improved Brutal Strike
description: Passive upgrade; your Brutal Strike extra damage becomes 2d10, and when you use Brutal Strike you can apply two different Brutal Strike effects to the same hit.

name: Indomitable Might
description: Passive; if your total for a Strength check or a Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use your Strength score in place of the total.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for), representing endgame-level power.

name: Danger Sense
description: Passive; you have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition. (This is your ‘sixth sense’ for dodging hazards.)

name: Reckless Attack
description: No action; when you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can choose to attack recklessly. Effect: you have Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, and attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

name: Primal Champion
description: Passive; your Strength and Constitution scores each increase by 4, and their maximums become 25.

name: Primal Knowledge
description: Passive; gain proficiency in one additional Barbarian skill. While your Rage is active, when you make an ability check using Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival, you can treat it as a Strength check (using your Strength modifier) instead of its normal ability.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat, or another feat you qualify for. You gain this feature again at Barbarian levels 8, 12, and 16.

name: Extra Attack
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

name: Fast Movement
description: Passive; while you aren’t wearing Heavy Armor, your Speed increases by 10 feet.

name: Feral Instinct
description: Passive; you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

name: Instinctive Pounce
description: No extra action; when you enter your Rage (as part of the same Bonus Action), you can move up to half your Speed immediately.

name: Brutal Strike
description: Trigger: on your turn, after you choose Reckless Attack, you may forgo Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll (the roll must not have Disadvantage). If that attack hits, it deals +1d10 extra damage of the same type and you apply one Brutal Strike effect: (1) Forceful Blow—push the target 15 ft straight away, then you may move up to half your Speed straight toward it without provoking Opportunity Attacks; or (2) Hamstring Blow—reduce the target’s Speed by 15 ft until the start of your next turn (a creature can be affected by only one Hamstring at a time).

name: Retaliation
description: Reaction; trigger: you take damage from a creature within 5 ft. Effect: make one melee attack against that creature (weapon or Unarmed Strike).

name: Intimidating Presence
description: Bonus Action; 30 ft emanation; choose creatures in the area. Each chosen creature makes a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + Proficiency Bonus). On a fail: Frightened for 1 minute; it repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending on a success. Uses: 1 per Long Rest, but after using it you can restore your use by expending 1 Rage use (no action required).

name: Frenzy
description: Trigger: while your Rage is active, if you use Reckless Attack, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. Extra damage = roll a number of d6 equal to your Rage Damage bonus; damage type matches the weapon or Unarmed Strike.

name: Mindless Rage
description: Passive; while your Rage is active, you are immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions. If you are Charmed or Frightened when you enter Rage, that condition ends on you immediately.

name: Nature Speaker
description: Passive; you can cast Commune with Nature, but only as a ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

name: Power of the Wilds
description: Trigger: whenever you activate your Rage, choose one option: (1) Falcon—while raging and wearing no armor, you gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed; (2) Lion—while raging, enemies within 5 ft of you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you (or another Barbarian with Lion active); (3) Ram—while raging, when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee attack, you can cause it to be Prone.

name: Animal Speaker
description: Passive; you can cast Beast Sense and Speak with Animals, but only as rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

name: Rage of the Wilds
description: Trigger: whenever you activate your Rage, choose one option: (1) Bear—while raging, you have Resistance to all damage types except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant; (2) Eagle—when you Rage you can Disengage and Dash as part of the same Bonus Action, and while raging you can take a Bonus Action to Disengage and Dash; (3) Wolf—while raging, your allies have Advantage on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 ft of you.

name: Aspect of the Wilds
description: Passive; choose one option (changeable on a Long Rest): Owl—Darkvision 60 ft (or +60 ft if you already have it); Panther—Climb Speed equal to your Speed; Salmon—Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

name: Battering Boots
description: Passive; during your turn, your reach is increased by 10 ft with melee weapons that have the Heavy or Versatile property. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can also apply the Push or Topple mastery property in addition to whatever other mastery property you’re using with that weapon.

name: Travel along the Tree
description: Teleportation; when you activate your Rage, and also as a Bonus Action while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. Once per Rage, you can extend one such teleport to 150 ft; when you do, you can also bring up to six willing creatures within 10 ft of you, each arriving in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 ft of your destination.

name: Vitality of the Tree
description: Trigger: when you activate your Rage, you gain Temporary HP equal to your Barbarian level. Also, at the start of each of your turns while raging, you can choose another creature within 10 ft to gain Temporary HP = roll a number of d6 equal to your Rage Damage bonus; any Temp HP from this feature vanish when your Rage ends.

name: Branches of the Tree
description: Reaction; trigger: a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 ft while your Rage is active. The target makes a Strength save (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + Proficiency Bonus). On a fail: it is teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 ft of you (or the nearest unoccupied space you can see), and after it teleports you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

name: Zealous Presence
description: Bonus Action; unleash a divine battle cry. Up to 10 other creatures of your choice within 60 ft that can hear you gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Rage of the Gods
description: While your Rage is active, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. In addition, at the start of each of your turns while raging, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 ft; it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or take Radiant damage equal to 1d6 + half your Barbarian level (rounded down).

name: Divine Fury
description: While your Rage is active, on each of your turns the first creature you hit with a weapon attack or an Unarmed Strike takes extra damage = 1d6 + half your Barbarian level (rounded down). Choose Radiant or Necrotic each time you deal this extra damage.

name: Warrior of the Gods
description: If a spell’s only effect is to restore you to life (not undeath), the caster doesn’t need material components to cast that spell on you.

name: Fanatical Focus
description: Once per Rage, if you fail a saving throw while your Rage is active, you can reroll it. You must use the new roll.

name: Leading Evasion
description: When an effect allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half damage on a failure. If any creatures within 5 ft of you are making the same Dexterity saving throw, you can share this benefit with them for that save. You can’t use this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Dazzling Footwork
description: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield: (1) Dance Virtuoso—Advantage on Charisma (Performance) checks that involve dancing; (2) Unarmored Defense—base AC = 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier; (3) Agile Strikes—when you expend a use of Bardic Inspiration as part of an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction, you can also make 1 Unarmed Strike as part of that same action/Bonus Action/Reaction; (4) Bardic Damage—you can use Dexterity for the attack rolls of your Unarmed Strikes, and when you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to your Bardic Inspiration die + your Dexterity modifier instead of the strike’s normal damage (this roll does NOT expend the die).

name: Inspiring Movement
description: Reaction; trigger: an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 ft of you. Cost: expend 1 use of Bardic Inspiration. Effect: you move up to half your Speed, and one ally of your choice within 30 ft also moves up to half their Speed using their Reaction. None of this movement provokes Opportunity Attacks.

name: Tandem Footwork
description: When you roll Initiative, if you don’t have the Incapacitated condition you can expend 1 use of Bardic Inspiration and roll your Bardic Inspiration die. You and each ally within 30 ft who can see or hear you gains a bonus to Initiative equal to the number rolled.

name: Unbreakable Majesty
description: Bonus Action; self; 1 minute (ends early if incapacitated). Creatures of your choice that can see you within 60 ft must make a Charisma save vs your spell save DC when they attempt to attack you; on a failure, they cannot target you with attacks that turn (they may choose other targets). On a success, they are immune for 24 hours. While active, you have Advantage on Charisma checks. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Mantle of Inspiration
description: Bonus Action; 60 ft; choose a number of creatures you can see up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Cost: expend one Bardic Inspiration die. Each chosen creature gains Temporary HP equal to the die + your Charisma modifier and can immediately use its Reaction to move up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

name: Enthralling Performance
description: Setup: perform for at least 1 minute and have creatures watch/listen. After the performance, choose a number of humanoids within 60 ft who watched you (usually up to your Charisma modifier). Each target makes a Wisdom save vs your spell save DC. On a failure: it is Charmed by you for 1 hour; the charm ends early if you or your allies harm it. On a success: it is unaffected (often with temporary immunity to this feature). Uses: 1 per Short or Long Rest.

name: Peerless Skill
description: No action; trigger: you make an ability check. Cost: expend one Bardic Inspiration die. Effect: add the die to the check after you roll the d20 but before the DM announces success/failure.

name: Cutting Words
description: Reaction; 60 ft; trigger: a creature you can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or damage roll. Cost: expend one Bardic Inspiration die. Effect: roll the die and subtract it from the triggering roll (apply to the roll you chose). Timing: after the creature rolls but before the outcome is resolved.

name: Additional Magical Secrets
description: Passive; you learn extra spells of your choice from any class list (beyond what Magical Secrets grants). These spells count as Bard spells for you, use Charisma, and are always prepared (or added to your prepared options, depending on your spell-prep rules).

name: Battle Magic
description: Trigger: you cast a Bard spell that takes 1 Action to cast. Effect: you can make one weapon attack as a Bonus Action immediately after the spell is cast. Limit: still only one Bonus Action per turn.

name: Combat Inspiration
description: Passive upgrade to your Bardic Inspiration. A creature that has your Bardic Inspiration can use it to add to a weapon damage roll (after it hits) or use its Reaction to add the die to AC against one attack (potentially turning a hit into a miss). Timing: declared after the attack roll is made but before damage is rolled.

name: Extra Attack
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

name: Bardic Inspiration
description: Bonus Action; 60 ft; choose one creature that can see or hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (a creature can have only one at a time). Within the next 1 hour, when it fails a D20 Test (attack roll, ability check, or saving throw), it can roll the die and add it to the d20, potentially turning failure into success; the die is expended when rolled. Uses: a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest. Die size: d6 at Bard 1–4, d8 at 5–9, d10 at 10–14, d12 at 15–20.

name: Ritual Casting
description: You can cast a prepared Bard spell as a ritual if it has the ritual tag, increasing its casting time by 10 minutes but expending no spell slot.

name: Spellcasting Focus
description: You can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus for Bard spells, replacing material components without a gold cost and not consumed.

name: Spellcasting
description: You prepare Bard spells and cast them using spell slots. Spellcasting ability: Charisma (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). Cantrips: you know 2 cantrips at Bard 1; you learn 1 additional Bard cantrip at Bard levels 4 and 10; whenever you gain a Bard level, you may replace one known Bard cantrip with another. Prepared Spells (level 1+): at Bard 1, prepare 4 level-1 Bard spells; whenever your Prepared Spells number increases, add new Bard spells of levels you have spell slots for; whenever you gain a Bard level, you may replace one prepared spell with another Bard spell you have slots for.

name: Magical Secrets
description: Passive; whenever you reach a Bard level (starting at 10) and your Prepared Spells number increases, you may choose any of those new prepared spells from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell lists; any spell chosen this way counts as a Bard spell for you. Also, whenever you replace a prepared spell when you gain a Bard level, you may replace it with a spell from those same four lists (it also counts as a Bard spell for you).

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Superior Inspiration
description: Passive; when you roll Initiative, regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have 2 uses, but only if you had fewer than 2 when Initiative was rolled.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for), representing endgame-level power.

name: Expertise
description: Choose two skill proficiencies; your Proficiency Bonus is doubled for checks using those skills.

name: Jack of All Trades
description: Passive; add half your Proficiency Bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn’t otherwise include your Proficiency Bonus.

name: Words of Creation
description: Passive; you always have Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if the second target is within 10 feet of the first.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Font of Inspiration
description: Passive upgrade; you regain all expended Bardic Inspiration uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Also, you can expend 1 spell slot (no action required) to regain 1 expended use of Bardic Inspiration.

name: Countercharm
description: Reaction; trigger: you or a creature within 30 ft fails a saving throw against an effect that would apply the Charmed or Frightened condition. Effect: the creature rerolls that saving throw, and the reroll has Advantage.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Expertise
description: Passive; choose two skill proficiencies; your Proficiency Bonus is doubled for checks using those skills.

name: Divine Order
description: Choose one: (1) Protector—gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Heavy armor; or (2) Thaumaturge—learn 1 extra Cleric cantrip, and add your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) to Intelligence (Arcana) and Intelligence (Religion) checks.

name: Ritual Casting
description: You can cast any prepared Cleric spell as a ritual if it has the Ritual tag (casting time increases by 10 minutes; no spell slot expended).

name: Spellcasting Focus
description: You can use a Holy Symbol as a spellcasting focus for your Cleric spells (replaces material components with no gold cost and not consumed).

name: Spellcasting
description: You prepare Cleric spells and cast them using spell slots. Spellcasting ability: Wisdom (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). Cantrips: you know 3 Cleric cantrips at Cleric 1; whenever you gain a Cleric level, you may replace one known Cleric cantrip with another; you learn additional cantrips at higher Cleric levels per the class table. Prepared Spells (level 1+): at Cleric 1, prepare 4 level-1 Cleric spells; whenever your Prepared Spells number increases, add new Cleric spells of levels you have spell slots for; whenever you gain a Cleric level, you may replace one prepared spell with another Cleric spell you have slots for.

name: Divine Intervention
description: Magic action; choose any Cleric spell of 5th level or lower that doesn’t require a Reaction to cast. You cast it without expending a spell slot and without Material components. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Improved Blessed Strikes
description: Passive; improves your Blessed Strikes choice: Divine Strike becomes +2d8 extra damage; Potent Spellcasting—when you deal damage with a Cleric cantrip, you can grant Temporary HP equal to 2 × your Wisdom modifier to you or one creature within 60 ft (no action).

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Channel Divinity
description: You can use Channel Divinity to fuel divine effects. Uses: 2; regain 1 expended use on a Short Rest and all on a Long Rest (more uses at higher Cleric levels per the class table). Channel Divinity save DC = your Cleric spell save DC.

name: Divine Spark
description: Magic Action; 30 ft; choose one creature you can see. Roll 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier; choose: Heal—the target regains HP equal to the total; or Harm—the target makes a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC; on a fail it takes Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice) equal to the total, on a success it takes half damage (rounded down). Scaling: roll 2d8 at Cleric 7, 3d8 at Cleric 13, 4d8 at Cleric 18 (still add Wisdom modifier once).

name: Turn Undead
description: Magic Action; 30 ft emanation from you; each Undead of your choice in the area makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, it is Frightened and has the Incapacitated condition for 1 minute and must spend its turns trying to move as far from you as it can. Ends early if it takes any damage, if you become Incapacitated, or if you die.

name: Greater Divine Intervention
description: Passive; when you use Divine Intervention, you can choose Wish as the spell. If you do, you can’t use Divine Intervention again until you finish 2d4 Long Rests.

name: Cleric Subclass
description: Passive; you choose a Cleric Domain. You gain that domain’s features at Cleric levels 3, 6, 10, and 17.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Sear Undead
description: Passive; when you use Turn Undead, each Undead that fails its save also takes Radiant damage. Damage: roll a number of d8s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1d8) and total them; each failed-save Undead takes that total. This damage does not end the Turn Undead effect.

name: Blessed Strikes
description: Passive; choose one option (you keep it unless you change it when you gain a Cleric level): (1) Divine Strike—once on each of your turns when you hit with a weapon attack roll, deal +1d8 extra Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice); or (2) Potent Spellcasting—add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Cleric cantrip.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Supreme Healing
description: Passive; when you would roll dice to restore HP with a spell or Channel Divinity, you instead use the maximum value for each healing die (no rolling).

name: Disciple of Life
description: Passive; when a spell you cast using a spell slot restores HP to a creature, that creature regains extra HP on the same turn equal to 2 + the spell slot’s level.

name: Life Domain Spells
description: Always prepared once you reach the listed Cleric level: 3—Bless, Cure Wounds; 5—Lesser Restoration, Spiritual Weapon; 7—Beacon of Hope, Revivify; 9—Death Ward, Guardian of Faith.

name: Preserve Life
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; choose any number of Bloodied creatures within 30 ft (can include you). You have a healing pool equal to 5 × your Cleric level; divide those HP among the chosen creatures. A creature can’t be healed by this feature above half its HP maximum.

name: Blessed Healer
description: Passive; immediately after you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores HP to one or more creatures other than you, you regain HP equal to 2 + the spell slot’s level.

name: Corona of Light
description: Magic action; self; lasts 1 minute or until you dismiss it (no action). Bright light 60 ft, dim light 30 ft beyond. Hostile creatures in the bright light have Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells that deal Fire or Radiant damage. Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Light Domain Spells
description: Always prepared once you reach the listed Cleric level: 3—Burning Hands, Faerie Fire; 5—Flaming Sphere, Scorching Ray; 7—Daylight, Fireball; 9—Guardian of Faith, Wall of Fire.

name: Radiance of the Dawn
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; 30 ft emanation from you. Dispel magical darkness in the area. Each hostile creature of your choice in the area makes a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC; on a fail it takes Radiant damage equal to 2d10 + your Cleric level, on a success it takes half damage (rounded down). Creatures behind total cover aren’t affected.

name: Warding Flare
description: Reaction; trigger: a creature you can see within 30 ft makes an attack roll. Effect: impose Disadvantage on that attack roll. Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Improved Warding Flare
description: Passive; when you use Warding Flare, you can target an attack roll made against you or a creature within 30 ft that you can see. (Same Reaction, same uses.)

name: Improved Duplicity
description: Passive; upgrade to Invoke Duplicity: Shared Distraction—when you and your allies make attack rolls against a creature within 5 ft of the illusion, those attack rolls have Advantage. Healing Illusion—when the illusion ends, you or one creature of your choice within 5 ft of it regains HP equal to your Cleric level.

name: Blessing of the Trickster
description: Magic Action; choose yourself or one willing creature within 30 ft. Target gains Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. Duration: until you finish a Long Rest or you use this feature again.

name: Invoke Duplicity
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; create an intangible visual illusion of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 ft. Duration: 1 minute; ends early if you dismiss it (no action) or you become Incapacitated. While it lasts: (1) Cast Spells—you can cast spells as though you were in the illusion’s space; (2) Distract—if both you and the illusion are within 5 ft of a creature that can see the illusion, you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature; (3) Move—Bonus Action to move the illusion up to 30 ft to a space you can see within 120 ft of you.

name: Trickery Domain Spells
description: Always prepared once you reach the listed Cleric level: 3—Charm Person, Disguise Self; 5—Mirror Image, Pass without Trace; 7—Blink, Dispel Magic; 9—Dimension Door, Polymorph.

name: Trickster’s Transposition
description: Passive; whenever you take the Bonus Action to create or move your Invoke Duplicity illusion, you can also teleport, swapping places with the illusion.

name: Avatar of Battle
description: You gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

name: Guided Strike
description: When you or a creature within 30 ft misses with an attack roll, you can expend 1 use of Channel Divinity to give that roll a +10 bonus, potentially turning it into a hit. If you use this feature to benefit another creature’s attack roll, you must use your Reaction.

name: War Domain Spells
description: Always prepared once you reach the listed Cleric level: 3—Guiding Bolt, Magic Weapon, Shield of Faith, Spiritual Weapon; 5—Crusader’s Mantle, Spirit Guardians; 7—Fire Shield, Freedom of Movement; 9—Hold Monster, Steel Wind Strike.

name: War Priest
description: Bonus Action; make one weapon attack or one Unarmed Strike. Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Regain all uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

name: War God’s Blessing
description: You can expend 1 use of Channel Divinity to cast Shield of Faith or Spiritual Weapon without expending a spell slot. When cast this way, the spell doesn’t require Concentration and instead lasts 1 minute; it ends early if you cast that spell again, if you have the Incapacitated condition, or if you die.

name: Nature’s Ward
description: Passive; you are immune to the Poisoned condition. Also, you gain Resistance based on your current land type: Arid—Fire; Polar—Cold; Temperate—Lightning; Tropical—Poison.

name: Nature’s Sanctuary
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape; create spectral trees and vines in a 15 ft Cube on the ground within 120 ft. Duration: 1 minute; ends early if you become Incapacitated or die. You and your allies have Half Cover while in the Cube, and your allies also gain your current Nature’s Ward Resistance while there. Bonus Action: move the Cube up to 60 ft to other ground within 120 ft.

name: Circle of the Land Spells
description: Each day, you manifest the wild plurality of the Lands you belong to (Arid, Polar, Temperate, and Tropical). You then have the Circle Spells for your land type prepared for your Druid level and lower. (These spells count as Druid spells for you and don’t count against your prepared spells.)

name: Land’s Aid
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape; choose a point within 60 ft. In a 10 ft-radius Sphere centered there, each creature of your choice makes a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC; on a fail it takes 2d6 Necrotic damage, on a success it takes half damage (rounded down). Also, one creature of your choice in the Sphere regains 2d6 HP. Scaling: damage and healing become 3d6 at Druid 10 and 4d6 at Druid 14.

name: Natural Recovery
description: Once per Long Rest, you can cast one prepared Circle of the Land spell (level 1+) from your Circle of the Land Spells feature without expending a spell slot. Also, when you finish a Short Rest, you can recover expended spell slots with a combined level up to half your Druid level (rounded up); none of the recovered slots can be 6th level or higher. Once you recover slots this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Moonlight Step
description: Bonus Action; teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you can see; you have Advantage on the next attack roll you make before the end of the turn.

name: Lunar Form
description: Passive; once per turn when you hit a creature with a Wild Shape attack, you deal an extra 2d10 Radiant damage. Also, when you use Moonlight Step, you can bring one willing creature within 5 ft of you to an unoccupied space within 5 ft of your destination.

name: Circle Forms
description: When you assume a Wild Shape form, you can enhance it with lunar magic: Challenge Rating—maximum CR equals your Druid level ÷ 3 (round down); Armor Class—your AC becomes 13 + your Wisdom modifier if that is higher than the form’s AC; Temporary Hit Points—when you use Wild Shape, your temporary HP from Wild Shape becomes 3 × your Druid level (instead of your Druid level).

name: Circle of the Moon Spells
description: You always have these spells prepared when you reach the listed Druid levels: 3—Cure Wounds, Moonbeam, Starry Wisp; 5—Conjure Animals; 7—Fount of Moonlight; 9—Mass Cure Wounds. You can cast these spells while you are in Wild Shape.

name: Improved Circle Forms
description: While in Wild Shape: Lunar Radiance—each time you hit with a Wild Shape attack, it can deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage (your choice per hit). Increased Toughness—you add your Wisdom modifier to Constitution saving throws.

name: Stormborn
description: Passive while Wrath of the Sea is active: you gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed, and you have Resistance to Cold, Lightning, and Thunder damage.

name: Oceanic Gift
description: When you use Wrath of the Sea, you can manifest the aura around one willing creature within 60 ft instead of around yourself; that creature uses your spell save DC and Wisdom modifier for the aura. Also, if you expend 2 Wild Shape uses when manifesting the aura, you can manifest it around both yourself and that willing creature at the same time.

name: Circle of the Sea Spells
description: You always have these spells prepared when you reach the listed Druid levels: 3—Fog Cloud, Gust of Wind, Ray of Frost, Shatter, Thunderwave; 5—Lightning Bolt, Water Breathing; 7—Control Water, Ice Storm; 9—Conjure Elemental, Hold Monster.

name: Wrath of the Sea
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape; manifest an ocean-spray aura for 10 minutes (ends early if you’re Incapacitated, you dismiss it, or you manifest it again). At the end of each of your turns, choose one creature you can see within 10 ft; it makes a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or takes Thunder damage equal to a number of d6s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1d6) and, if Large or smaller, is pushed up to 15 ft away from you.

name: Aquatic Affinity
description: Passive; the reach of Wrath of the Sea becomes 10 ft. Also, you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

name: Twinkling Constellations
description: Starry Form improves: Archer and Chalice’s 1d8 becomes 2d8. While Dragon is active, you gain a Fly Speed of 20 ft and can hover. Also, at the start of each of your turns while in Starry Form, you can switch which constellation is active.

name: Full of Stars
description: While in Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal and gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

name: Circle of the Stars Spells
description: You always have these spells prepared when you reach the listed Druid levels: 3—Cure Wounds, Moonbeam, Starry Wisp; 5—Conjure Animals; 7—Fount of Moonlight; 9—Mass Cure Wounds. You can cast these spells while you are in Wild Shape.

name: Star Map
description: You create a star chart (Tiny object) that you can use as a spellcasting focus for your Druid spells. While holding it, you have Guidance and Guiding Bolt prepared. You can cast Guiding Bolt without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), regaining all expended free casts on a Long Rest. If you lose the map, you can recreate it with a 1-hour ceremony during a Short or Long Rest; this destroys the previous map.

name: Starry Form
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape to assume a luminous starry form for 10 minutes (ends early if you dismiss it, you’re Incapacitated, or you use it again). It sheds bright light 10 ft and dim light 10 ft beyond. Choose a constellation when you assume the form: Archer—when you activate the form and as a Bonus Action on later turns, make a ranged spell attack vs a creature within 60 ft; on hit, deal Radiant damage = 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier. Chalice—when you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores HP to a creature, you or another creature within 30 ft regains HP = 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier. Dragon—when you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check, or a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration, treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

name: Cosmic Omen
description: After each Long Rest, roll a die to determine an omen until your next Long Rest: Weal (even)—Reaction when a creature you can see within 30 ft is about to make a d20 Test; roll 1d6 and add it to the total. Woe (odd)—same trigger, but subtract 1d6 from the total. Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1); regain all uses on a Long Rest.

name: Druidic
description: You know Druidic. Also, you always have Speak with Animals prepared (it doesn’t count against your prepared spells).

name: Primal Order
description: Choose one: (1) Warden—gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor; or (2) Magician—learn 1 extra Druid cantrip, and add your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) to Intelligence (Arcana) and Intelligence (Nature) checks.

name: Ritual Casting
description: You can cast any prepared Druid spell as a ritual if it has the Ritual tag (casting time increases by 10 minutes; no spell slot expended).

name: Spellcasting Focus
description: You can use a Druidic Focus as a spellcasting focus for your Druid spells (replaces material components with no gold cost and not consumed).

name: Spellcasting
description: You prepare Druid spells and cast them using spell slots. Spellcasting ability: Wisdom (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). Cantrips: you know 2 Druid cantrips at Druid 1; whenever you gain a Druid level, you may replace one known Druid cantrip with another. Prepared Spells (level 1+): you prepare a fixed number of Druid spells by Druid level (per class table); whenever you gain a Druid level, you can change which Druid spells you have prepared.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Improved Elemental Fury
description: Passive; improves your Elemental Fury choice: Primal Strike becomes +2d8 extra elemental damage; Potent Spellcasting—when you deal damage with a Druid cantrip, you can grant Temporary HP equal to 2 × your Wisdom modifier to you or one creature within 60 ft (no action).

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Beast Spells
description: Passive; while in Wild Shape, you can cast spells, and you can provide material components that have no listed cost and aren’t consumed by the spell.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Wild Companion
description: Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape to cast Find Familiar with no material components. The familiar is Fey (not Beast) and disappears after a number of hours equal to half your Druid level (rounded down).

name: Wild Shape
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Wild Shape to assume a Beast form you know (you keep your game statistics; you can’t cast spells). You retain your current HP and gain Temporary HP equal to your Druid level when you transform. You can speak in Beast form. Uses: 2 per Long Rest at Druid 2; 3 per Long Rest at Druid 6; 4 per Long Rest at Druid 17. Regain 1 expended use when you finish a Short Rest; regain all on a Long Rest. Known Forms: you know 4 Beast forms at Druid 2; you learn 2 more at Druid 4 (6 total) and 2 more at Druid 6 (8 total); when you finish a Long Rest you can replace one known form with another eligible Beast form.

name: Archdruid
description: Passive; Evergreen Wild Shape—when you roll Initiative, if you have no Wild Shape uses remaining, you regain 1 use. Nature Magician—when you expend a Wild Shape use, you can instead choose to restore expended spell slots; each Wild Shape use restores spell slots with a combined level of 2 (for example, one 2nd-level slot, or two 1st-level slots), up to the normal limits of your spell slots.

name: Druid Subclass
description: Passive; you choose a Druid Circle. You gain that circle’s features at Druid levels 3, 6, 10, and 14.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Wild Resurgence
description: Resource swap; once per Long Rest, you can expend 1 use of Wild Shape to regain one expended 1st-level spell slot. Also, you can expend a spell slot to regain 1 expended use of Wild Shape (no action required).

name: Elemental Fury
description: Passive; choose one option (you keep it unless you change it when you gain a Druid level): (1) Primal Strike—once on each of your turns when you hit with a weapon attack roll or a Beast-form attack roll, deal +1d8 extra Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you gain the feature); or (2) Potent Spellcasting—add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Improved Combat Superiority
description: Passive; your Superiority Dice become d10s.

name: Relentless
description: Passive; once per turn when you use a maneuver, you can use a d8 in place of expending a Superiority Die.

name: Ultimate Combat Superiority
description: Passive; your Superiority Dice become d12s.

name: Combat Superiority
description: You learn battle maneuvers fueled by Superiority Dice. Dice: 4d8 at level 3; you regain all expended dice when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Save DC for maneuvers that require one = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice when you gain this feature). You learn 3 maneuvers at level 3; you learn 2 more at level 7, 2 more at level 10, and 2 more at level 15.

name: Student of War
description: Passive; gain proficiency in one skill of your choice and proficiency with one set of artisan’s tools of your choice.

name: Know Your Enemy
description: Action; choose one creature you can see within 30 ft. You learn whether it has any damage resistances, damage immunities, or damage vulnerabilities (you learn the categories, not necessarily the full stat block). Uses: 1 per Long Rest; after you use it, you can use it again by expending 1 Superiority Die (no action required to ‘pay’ the die; still costs the Action to use the feature).

name: Heroic Warrior
description: Passive; at the start of each of your turns in combat, if you don’t have Heroic Inspiration, you gain it.

name: Superior Critical
description: Passive; your weapon attacks and Unarmed Strikes score a critical hit on a d20 roll of 18–20.

name: Survivor
description: Passive; you have Advantage on death saving throws, and when you roll an 18–20 on a death saving throw it counts as a critical success (regain 1 HP).

name: Improved Critical
description: Passive; your weapon attacks and Unarmed Strikes score a critical hit on a d20 roll of 19–20.

name: Additional Fighting Style
description: Passive; you gain a second Fighting Style feat of your choice.

name: Remarkable Athlete
description: Passive; you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Strength (Athletics) checks. Also, when you score a critical hit, you can move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

name: Fighting Style
description: Passive; you gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice.

name: Second Wind
description: Bonus Action; regain HP = 1d10 + your Fighter level. Uses: 2 (Fighter levels 1–3), 3 (levels 4–9), 4 (levels 10–20). Regain 1 expended use when you finish a Short Rest; regain all uses when you finish a Long Rest.

name: Weapon Mastery
description: Passive; you can use the mastery properties of three kinds of Simple or Martial weapons you choose. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those chosen weapon kinds. Number of weapon kinds you can master: 3 (Fighter levels 1–3), 4 (levels 4–9), 5 (levels 10–15), 6 (levels 16–20).

name: Second Wind Improvement
description: Passive; your number of Second Wind uses becomes 4 (and remains 4 through level 20).

name: Two Extra Attacks
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make three attacks instead of one.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Studied Attacks
description: Passive; if you make an attack roll against a creature and miss, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Action Surge Improvement
description: Passive; you can now use Action Surge twice between Short or Long Rests (but only once on the same turn). Also, your Indomitable uses become 3 per Long Rest.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Action Surge
description: No action (on your turn); take one additional Action, except the Magic action. Uses: 1 per Short or Long Rest; starting at Fighter 17 you can use it twice between rests (but only once on the same turn).

name: Tactical Mind
description: Trigger: you fail an ability check. Cost: you may expend 1 use of Second Wind (instead of healing). Effect: roll 1d10 and add it to the failed check, potentially turning failure into success. If the check still fails, the Second Wind use is not expended.

name: Three Extra Attacks
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make four attacks instead of one.

name: Fighter Subclass
description: Passive; you choose a Fighter subclass. You gain that subclass’s features at Fighter levels 3, 7, 10, 15, and 18.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for. (You gain this feature again at Fighter levels 6, 8, 12, 14, and 16.)

name: Extra Attack
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

name: Tactical Shift
description: No extra action; whenever you activate Second Wind with a Bonus Action, you can move up to half your Speed immediately as part of the same Bonus Action, and this movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Indomitable
description: Trigger (no action required); when you fail a saving throw, reroll it and add a bonus equal to your Fighter level. You must use the new roll. Uses: 1 per Long Rest at Fighter 9; 2 per Long Rest at Fighter 13; 3 per Long Rest at Fighter 17.

name: Tactical Master
description: Passive; when you make an attack with a weapon whose mastery property you can use, you can replace that weapon’s mastery property for that attack with Push, Sap, or Slow.

name: Eldritch Strike
description: Passive; when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

name: Arcane Charge
description: No action; when you use Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

name: Improved War Magic
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a Wizard spell of 1st or 2nd level that has a casting time of 1 Action.

name: Spellcasting
description: You learn spells from the Wizard spell list and cast them using Intelligence (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). Cantrips: you learn 2 Wizard cantrips at level 3; when you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one known cantrip with another Wizard cantrip. You learn and prepare a limited number of Wizard spells as shown on the Eldritch Knight table.

name: War Bond
description: Ritual; bond with one weapon (you can have up to two bonded weapons). While bonded: you can’t be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the Incapacitated condition. Bonus Action: summon one bonded weapon to your hand if it is on the same plane. You can’t bond a weapon already bonded to another Fighter or attuned to another creature.

name: War Magic
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a Wizard cantrip that has a casting time of 1 Action.

name: Guarded Mind
description: Passive; you have Resistance to Psychic damage. When you start your turn Charmed or Frightened, you can expend 1 Psionic Energy die to end one of those conditions on yourself.

name: Bulwark of Force
description: Bonus Action; choose creatures within 30 ft up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). For 1 minute, they gain Half Cover (+2 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws). Uses: 1 per Long Rest unless you expend 1 Psionic Energy die to use it again.

name: Telekinetic Master
description: You always have Telekinesis prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot; regain the free cast on a Long Rest. When you cast it, you can also make one weapon attack as a Bonus Action on each of your turns for the spell’s duration.

name: Psionic Power
description: You gain Psionic Energy dice (d6s). Dice: 4 at level 3; you regain all expended dice on a Long Rest and regain 1 die when you finish a Short Rest. Save DC (if required) = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier. You learn the following options: Protective Field (Reaction; reduce damage to a creature within 30 ft by 1 Psionic Energy die + your Intelligence modifier), Psionic Strike (once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, expend 1 die to deal extra Force damage equal to the die + your Intelligence modifier), and Telekinetic Movement (Action; move one object or willing creature within 30 ft up to 30 ft; once per Short or Long Rest unless you expend a die to use it again).

name: Telekinetic Adept
description: You gain two additional Psionic Power options: Psi-Powered Leap (Bonus Action; gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your walking Speed until end of turn; once per Short or Long Rest unless you expend a die), and Telekinetic Thrust (when you deal Psionic Strike damage, target makes a Strength save or is knocked Prone or pushed up to 10 ft). Also, your Psionic Energy dice become d8s.

name: Martial Arts
description: Passive; while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk Weapons and you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield: (1) Bonus Unarmed Strike—you can make an Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action; (2) Martial Arts Die—you can roll 1d6 instead of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk Weapons (die scales: d6 at Monk 1–4, d8 at 5–10, d10 at 11–16, d12 at 17–20); (3) Dexterous Attacks—you can use Dexterity for attack and damage rolls with Unarmed Strikes and Monk Weapons; and when you Grapple or Shove with an Unarmed Strike, you can use Dexterity to set the save DC. Monk Weapons: Simple melee weapons, and Martial melee weapons with the Light property.

name: Unarmored Defense
description: Passive; while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your base AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

name: Heightened Focus
description: Passive upgrades to Monk’s Focus options: Flurry of Blows—spend 1 FP to make 3 Unarmed Strikes (instead of 2). Patient Defense—when you spend 1 FP for Disengage + Dodge, also gain Temporary HP equal to 2 rolls of your Martial Arts die. Step of the Wind—when you spend 1 FP for Dash + Disengage, you can also choose a willing Large-or-smaller creature within 5 ft and move it with you until end of turn; that creature’s movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks.

name: Self-Restoration
description: Passive; at the end of each of your turns, you can remove one of these conditions from yourself: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned. Also, forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you levels of Exhaustion.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Deflect Energy
description: Passive; you can now use Deflect Attacks against attacks that deal any damage type (not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing).

name: Disciplined Survivor
description: Passive; you gain proficiency in all saving throws. Also, when you fail a saving throw, you can spend 1 Focus Point to reroll it (you must use the new roll).

name: Perfect Focus
description: Trigger: when you roll Initiative and you don’t use Uncanny Metabolism. Effect: regain expended Focus Points until you have 4, but only if you had 3 or fewer at the moment Initiative was rolled.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Superior Defense
description: At the start of your turn, you can spend 3 Focus Points to gain Resistance to all damage except Force for 1 minute (ends early if you have the Incapacitated condition).

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Monk’s Focus
description: You gain Focus Points (FP) as shown on the Monk class table; you regain all expended FP when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can spend FP to fuel: Flurry of Blows (1 FP; Bonus Action; make 2 Unarmed Strikes), Patient Defense (Bonus Action; Disengage; or 1 FP to Disengage + Dodge as the same Bonus Action), and Step of the Wind (Bonus Action; Dash; or 1 FP to Dash + Disengage as the same Bonus Action and your jump distance is doubled for the turn). Saving throw DC for Monk features = 8 + your Wisdom modifier + Proficiency Bonus.

name: Unarmored Movement
description: Passive; while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your Speed increases by 10 feet (bonus increases by level per the class table).

name: Uncanny Metabolism
description: Trigger: when you roll Initiative. Effect: regain all expended Focus Points, then roll your Martial Arts die and regain HP equal to your Monk level + the die roll. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Body and Mind
description: Passive; your Dexterity and Wisdom scores each increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

name: Deflect Attacks
description: Reaction; trigger: an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing. Reduce the attack’s total damage against you by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect force: choose a creature you can see within 5 ft of you (if the attack was melee) or within 60 ft of you that isn’t behind Total Cover (if the attack was ranged). That creature makes a Dexterity save vs your Monk save DC or takes damage equal to 2 rolls of your Martial Arts die + your Dexterity modifier; damage type matches the triggering attack.

name: Monk Subclass
description: Passive; choose a Monk subclass. You gain its features at Monk levels 3, 6, 11, and 17 (as listed by the subclass).

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, and 16.

name: Slow Fall
description: Reaction; trigger: you fall. Reduce falling damage you take by 5 × your Monk level.

name: Extra Attack
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

name: Stunning Strike
description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can spend 1 Focus Point to attempt to stun it. The target makes a Constitution saving throw vs your Monk save DC. On a failed save: it has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save: its Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against it before then has Advantage.

name: Empowered Strikes
description: Passive; whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, you can make it deal Force damage instead of its normal damage type.

name: Evasion
description: Passive; when an effect lets you make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and half damage on a failure. You don’t benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Acrobatic Movement
description: Passive; while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you can move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace each of the Unarmed Strikes with a use of Hand of Healing without spending additional Focus Points. Also, when you use Hand of Harm, you can deal extra Necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die instead of one.

name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
description: Action; you can touch a creature that died within the last 24 hours and expend 5 Focus Points to restore it to life with HP equal to 4 rolls of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Hand of Harm
description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal extra Necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier.

name: Hand of Healing
description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the Unarmed Strikes with a healing touch. A creature you touch regains HP equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier.

name: Implements of Mercy
description: You gain proficiency in Insight and Medicine, and proficiency with the Herbalism Kit.

name: Physician’s Touch
description: When you use Hand of Healing on a creature, you can also end one disease or one of the following conditions affecting it: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned. When you use Hand of Harm, the target also has the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

name: Improved Shadow Step
description: When you use Shadow Step, you can spend 1 Focus Point to ignore the requirement that you must start and end in Dim Light or Darkness for that use. As part of this Bonus Action, you can make an Unarmed Strike immediately after you teleport.

name: Cloak of Shadows
description: Magic Action; while entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, spend 3 Focus Points to shroud yourself in shadows for 1 minute (ends early if you become Incapacitated or if you end your turn in Bright Light). While shrouded: (1) you have the Invisible condition; (2) Partially Incorporeal—you can move through occupied spaces as Difficult Terrain (if you end your turn in one, you’re shunted to the last unoccupied space you occupied); (3) Shadow Flurry—you can use Flurry of Blows without spending Focus Points.

name: Shadow Arts
description: You gain three benefits: (1) Darkness—you can spend 1 Focus Point to cast Darkness with no components; you can see within the spell’s area when cast this way, and while it lasts you can move the area to a space within 60 ft of you at the start of each of your turns; (2) Darkvision—gain Darkvision 60 ft (or +60 ft if you already have it); (3) Shadowy Figments—you know Minor Illusion (Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it).

name: Shadow Step
description: Bonus Action; while entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, teleport up to 60 ft to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. You then have Advantage on the next melee attack you make before the end of the current turn.

name: Stride of the Elements
description: Passive; while your Elemental Attunement is active, you also have a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

name: Elemental Epitome
description: Passive; while your Elemental Attunement is active, you also gain: (1) Damage Resistance—Resistance to one of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder (choose; you can change the choice at the start of each of your turns); (2) Destructive Stride—when you use Step of the Wind, your Speed increases by 20 ft until end of turn, and for that duration any creature of your choice takes damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you enter a space within 5 ft of it (type: your choice of Acid/Cold/Fire/Lightning/Thunder; each creature can take this only once per turn); (3) Empowered Strikes—once on each of your turns when you hit with an Unarmed Strike, deal extra damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die (type matches the strike).

name: Elemental Attunement
description: At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 Focus Point to imbue yourself with elemental energy for 10 minutes (ends early if you become Incapacitated). While active: Reach—your Unarmed Strike reach is 10 feet greater than normal; Elemental Strikes—when you hit with an Unarmed Strike, you can make it deal Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage instead of its normal type, and when you deal one of those types you can force a Strength saving throw against your Monk save DC; on a failed save, you move the target up to 10 ft toward or away from you.

name: Manipulate Elements
description: Passive; you know the Elementalism spell, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

name: Elemental Burst
description: Magic Action; spend 2 Focus Points; choose a point within 120 ft and a damage type (Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder). In a 20 ft-radius Sphere centered there, each creature makes a Dexterity saving throw against your Monk save DC, taking damage equal to 3 rolls of your Martial Arts die on a failed save, or half damage on a success.

name: Fleet Step
description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that Bonus Action.

name: Quivering Palm
description: When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can spend 4 Focus Points to start imperceptible vibrations in it that last a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you end them: when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one attack to end them (you and the target must be on the same plane). When ended, the target makes a Constitution saving throw against your Monk save DC, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half damage on a successful one. Only one creature can be affected at a time; you can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action).

name: Open Hand Technique
description: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, choose one effect for that target: Addle—the target can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn; Push—the target makes a Strength saving throw against your Monk save DC or is pushed up to 15 ft away from you; Topple—the target makes a Dexterity saving throw against your Monk save DC or has the Prone condition.

name: Wholeness of Body
description: Bonus Action; roll your Martial Arts die and regain HP equal to the roll + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1 HP). Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1); regain all uses on a Long Rest.

name: Divine Sense
description: Bonus Action; 60 ft; until end of your next turn you know the location of any Celestial, Fiend, or Undead within range that isn’t behind Total Cover, and you detect any place or object within range that has been consecrated or desecrated. Uses: a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Lay on Hands
description: You have a healing pool equal to 5 × your Paladin level. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature and restore any number of HP from the pool (up to remaining pool). You can also expend 5 HP from the pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison affecting the target. The pool replenishes when you finish a Long Rest.

name: Weapon Mastery
description: Passive; you can use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons you choose. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one chosen weapon kind. Number of weapon kinds increases to 3 at Paladin 9 and 4 at Paladin 17.

name: Aura of Courage
description: Passive; you and friendly creatures within 10 ft of you can’t have the Frightened condition while you are conscious.

name: Radiant Strikes
description: Passive; once on each of your turns when you hit with a weapon attack, you deal an extra 1d8 Radiant damage.

name: Cleansing Touch
description: Action; touch a creature to end one spell on it. Uses: a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Divine Smite
description: Trigger: when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack. Effect: expend one spell slot to deal Radiant damage in addition to the weapon’s damage. Extra damage = 2d8 for a 1st‑level slot, +1d8 per slot level above 1st (maximum 5d8). If the target is a Fiend or Undead, add 1d8 more.

name: Fighting Style
description: Passive; you gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice.

name: Spellcasting
description: You prepare Paladin spells and cast them using spell slots. Spellcasting ability: Charisma (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). You prepare a fixed number of Paladin spells by Paladin level; whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can change which Paladin spells you have prepared.

name: Divine Champion
description: Passive; your Charisma score increases by 4 (maximum 25). In addition, when you roll Initiative and have no uses of Channel Divinity remaining, you regain 1 use.

name: Channel Divinity
description: You gain two Channel Divinity options from your Sacred Oath. Uses: 2; regain 1 expended use on a Short Rest and all on a Long Rest. Save DC = your Paladin spell save DC.

name: Sacred Oath
description: Passive; choose a Sacred Oath. You gain oath features at Paladin levels 3, 7, 15, and 20.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for. You gain this feature again at Paladin levels 8, 12, and 16.

name: Extra Attack
description: Passive; when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make two attacks instead of one.

name: Aura of Protection
description: Passive; while you are conscious, you and friendly creatures within 10 ft of you add your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to all saving throws. Range increases to 30 ft at Paladin 18.

name: Abjure Foes
description: Magic Action; 30 ft emanation; creatures of your choice make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, a creature is Frightened of you for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of each of its turns, ending on a success). Uses: once per Long Rest, or by expending a 3rd-level or higher spell slot.

name: Smite of Protection
description: Passive; when you use Divine Smite, you and one creature within 30 ft gain Temporary HP equal to your Charisma modifier + the spell slot level expended.

name: Holy Nimbus
description: Bonus Action; for 1 minute, Bright Light 30 ft and Dim 30 ft beyond. Enemies in Bright Light take Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier at the start of their turns. You have Advantage on saving throws against spells cast by Fiends and Undead. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Sacred Weapon
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity. For 1 minute, add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with one weapon you are holding; the weapon emits Bright Light 20 ft and Dim Light 20 ft beyond. Ends early if you release or fall unconscious.

name: Turn the Unholy
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; 30 ft emanation. Each Fiend or Undead of your choice makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure: Frightened for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of each turn).

name: Oath Spells
description: Always prepared once you reach the listed Paladin levels: 3—Sanctuary; 5—Lesser Restoration.

name: Aura of Devotion
description: Passive; you and friendly creatures within 10 ft of you can’t have the Charmed condition while you are conscious.

name: Glorious Defense
description: Reaction; trigger: you or a creature within 10 ft of you is hit by an attack roll. Effect: add your Charisma modifier to the target’s AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker. Uses: a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Glorious Defense
description: Reaction; when you or another creature within 10 ft is hit by an attack, add your Charisma modifier to the target’s AC against that attack. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker.

name: Living Legend
description: Bonus Action; for 1 minute, you gain Advantage on Charisma checks, can reroll one missed attack per turn, and can use your Reaction to reroll a failed saving throw. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Living Legend
description: Bonus Action; for 1 minute, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saving throws, and once on each of your turns when you miss with a weapon attack, you can cause that attack to hit instead. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Inspiring Smite
description: Bonus Action; immediately after you deal damage with Divine Smite, expend 1 use of Channel Divinity. Distribute Temporary HP equal to 2d8 + your Paladin level among creatures of your choice within 30 ft.

name: Inspiring Smite
description: Immediately after you deal damage with Divine Smite, you can distribute Temporary HP equal to 2d8 + your Paladin level among creatures of your choice within 30 ft (divide as you choose).

name: Peerless Athlete
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity. For 10 minutes, you have Advantage on Athletics and Acrobatics checks, can carry/push/drag twice as much, and your long/high jumps increase by 10 ft.

name: Peerless Athlete
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity. For 10 minutes, you have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, your carrying capacity doubles, and your jump distance is tripled.

name: Aura of Alacrity
description: Passive; your walking speed increases by 10 ft. Friendly creatures that start their turn within 5 ft of you have their speed increased by 10 ft until end of turn.

name: Aura of Alacrity
description: Passive; your Speed increases by 10 ft. Friendly creatures who start their turn within 10 ft of you also have their Speed increased by 10 ft until the end of that turn.

name: Undying Sentinel
description: Passive; when reduced to 0 HP and not killed outright, you drop to 1 HP instead (once per Long Rest). Also, you suffer no drawbacks from old age.

name: Elder Champion
description: Bonus Action; for 1 minute you regain 10 HP at the start of each of your turns, and enemy creatures within 10 ft have Disadvantage on saving throws against your Paladin spells and Channel Divinity effects. Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Nature’s Wrath
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; one creature within 30 ft must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (target’s choice) against your spell save DC or be Restrained by spectral vines for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of turns).

name: Turn the Faithless
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; 30 ft emanation. Each Fey or Fiend of your choice makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC; on a failure, Frightened for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of turns).

name: Aura of Warding
description: Passive; you and friendly creatures within 10 ft of you have Resistance to damage from spells while you are conscious.

name: Soul of Vengeance
description: Reaction; when a creature under your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you can make one melee weapon attack against it.

name: Avenging Angel
description: Bonus Action; for 1 hour you gain a Fly Speed of 60 ft and emit a 30 ft aura of menace. Enemies that start their turn in the aura make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or are Frightened for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of turns). Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Abjure Enemy
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; one creature within 60 ft makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure: Frightened for 1 minute and Speed reduced to 0; repeat saving throw at end of each turn. On a success: Speed halved for 1 minute.

name: Vow of Enmity
description: Bonus Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; choose one creature within 30 ft. For 1 minute, you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature. Ends early if the creature drops to 0 HP or you are Incapacitated.

name: Relentless Avenger
description: Passive; when you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, you can move up to half your Speed immediately after the attack without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

name: Supernatural Resistance
description: Passive; you have Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons.

name: Dread Lord
description: Bonus Action; for 1 minute, you emit an aura of darkness in a 30 ft radius. Enemies in the aura take Necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier at the start of their turns, and you can cause one creature to be Frightened of you (repeat saving throw at end of turns). Uses: 1 per Long Rest.

name: Control Undead
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; one Undead within 30 ft makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, it is Charmed by you for 24 hours.

name: Dreadful Aspect
description: Magic Action; expend 1 use of Channel Divinity; 30 ft emanation. Creatures of your choice make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure: Frightened for 1 minute (repeat saving throw at end of turns).

name: Aura of Hate
description: Passive; you and Fiends and Undead within 10 ft add your Charisma modifier to melee weapon damage rolls.

name: Bestial Fury
description: When you command your Primal Companion to take the Beast’s Strike action, it can use Beast’s Strike twice. Also, the first time each turn it hits a creature under the effect of your Hunter’s Mark, it deals extra Force damage equal to Hunter’s Mark’s bonus damage.

name: Share Spells
description: When you cast a spell that targets only yourself, you can also have it affect your Primal Companion if the beast is within 30 ft of you.

name: Primal Companion
description: You magically summon a primal beast companion (choose Beast of the Land, Sea, or Sky stat block). The companion is Friendly to you and obeys your commands. It acts on your turn: it moves and uses its Reaction normally, but takes only the Dodge action unless you use a Bonus Action to command it to take another action (including an action in its stat block). When you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to command the beast to take its Beast’s Strike action. If you’re Incapacitated, the beast acts on its own. If the beast died within the last hour, you can take a Magic action to touch it and expend a spell slot; it returns to life after 1 minute with all HP restored. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can summon a different primal beast; the old one vanishes. The beast vanishes if you die.

name: Exceptional Training
description: When you use your Bonus Action to command your Primal Companion to take an action, you can also command it to take Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help using its Bonus Action. Also, whenever it hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can deal Force damage instead of its normal damage type.

name: Favored Enemy
description: You always have Hunter’s Mark prepared. You can cast Hunter’s Mark without expending a spell slot 2 times, and you regain all expended free casts when you finish a Long Rest. The number of free casts increases at higher Ranger levels per the Ranger table.

name: Spellcasting
description: You prepare Ranger spells and cast them using spell slots. Spellcasting ability: Wisdom (spell save DC and spell attack bonus). Prepared Spells (level 1+): at Ranger 1, you prepare 2 level-1 Ranger spells; your prepared list size increases by Ranger level as shown on the Ranger table (e.g., 6 prepared spells at Ranger 5). Whenever the number increases, add new Ranger spells of levels you have slots for. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace 1 prepared Ranger spell with another Ranger spell you have slots for. Spellcasting Focus: you can use a Druidic Focus.

name: Weapon Mastery
description: Passive; you can use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons you choose that you’re proficient with (e.g., Longbows, Shortswords). Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the two chosen weapon kinds.

name: Tireless
description: You gain two benefits. Temporary Hit Points: Magic Action; give yourself Temporary HP equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest. Decrease Exhaustion: whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level (if any) decreases by 1.

name: Relentless Hunter
description: Passive; taking damage can’t break your Concentration on Hunter’s Mark.

name: Nature’s Veil
description: Bonus Action; you gain the Invisible condition until the end of your next turn. Uses: a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) per Long Rest.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for.

name: Precise Hunter
description: Passive; you have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your Hunter’s Mark.

name: Feral Senses
description: Passive; you gain Blindsight 30 ft.

name: Epic Boon
description: Passive; you gain an Epic Boon feat (or another feat you qualify for).

name: Deft Explorer
description: Passive; you gain Expertise in 1 skill proficiency you have (choose a skill you’re proficient with that you don’t already have Expertise in). You also learn 2 languages of your choice.

name: Fighting Style
description: Passive; you gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Alternative option: Druidic Warrior—learn 2 Druid cantrips of your choice; they count as Ranger spells for you and use Wisdom; whenever you gain a Ranger level, you may replace 1 of these cantrips with another Druid cantrip.

name: Foe Slayer
description: Passive; the bonus-damage die of your Hunter’s Mark becomes a d10 (instead of a d6).

name: Ranger Subclass
description: Passive; choose a Ranger subclass. You gain that subclass’s features at Ranger levels 3, 7, 11, and 15.

name: Ability Score Improvement
description: Passive; you gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat you qualify for. You gain this feature again at Ranger levels 8, 12, and 16.
